data.title: ServisFirst signals continued margin expansion and targets robust Texas growth in 2026
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ServisFirst Bancshares (SFBS) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Thomas Broughton indicated that loan growth for the quarter was in line with projections, reporting "annualized growth of 12% for the quarter." He noted pipeline increases and a diminishing payoff headwind, stating "the payoff headwind is diminishing to some extent" and expressed optimism for further improvement. Broughton highlighted ongoing management of high-cost municipal deposits and a strong deposit-gathering position, remarking that robust loan demand would allow the bank to "attract some of those type deposits back if they are needed."
  • Broughton announced the launch of a new Texas banking team based in Houston, describing the group as "very excited" and "productive in temporary office space already," with a current team of nine and more hires expected in the first two quarters of 2026. He emphasized that "this is a much larger team than we have hired in the recent past" and expressed high expectations for the region.
  • The company expanded its correspondent banking footprint, reaching 388 banks and growing its agent credit card program to 150 banks, with further endorsements from new state banking associations.
  • CFO David Sparacio stated, "we recorded $1.58 of earnings per diluted share for the fourth quarter, which is a 32% increase from the third quarter of 2025 and a 33% increase from the fourth quarter of 2024." Sparacio added, "net income available to common shareholders was $86.4 million for the quarter and $276.5 million for the year."
  • Sparacio reported that "our net interest margin experienced healthy growth throughout 2025, rising from 2.92% in the first quarter to 3.38% in the fourth quarter." He attributed the expansion to disciplined loan pricing and deposit rate reductions.
  • COO Rodney Rushing highlighted a "4%" increase in tangible book value per share to $33.62 during the quarter.

Outlook

  • Management expects the Texas team to drive significant growth in 2026, with Broughton stating, "we have great expectations from Texas" and that the group's "budgeted growth for 2026 is higher than any other region."
  • On margin, Sparacio told analysts, "using the December spot margin is a good starting point for 2026," and forecasted further expansion, supported by loan repricing: "we expect continued margin expansion...throughout 2026."
  • Regarding expense growth, Sparacio said, "we're thinking high single digit" for 2026, noting the impact of new hires but expecting efficiency ratio to remain in the low 30s.
  • Tax strategy will focus on maximizing credits, particularly "around solar credits," with Broughton stating, "we're going to continue to try to manage that down going forward."

Financial Results

  • Net interest margin rose to "3.38% in the fourth quarter," up from "2.92% in the first quarter."
  • Loan growth was "equally split between our C&I and real estate portfolios with about 10% annual growth in each."
  • Noninterest revenue increased with a "26% growth from full year 2024 to full year 2025" in service charges and an "11% annual increase in mortgage banking fee income."
  • Efficiency ratio fell below "30% for the quarter," with the full-year adjusted ratio at "near 32%, which is a 14% improvement over 2024."
  • Noninterest expense was "flat and down about 3% versus linked quarters," and up "only 2%" for the full year.
  • The company paid down "$30 million of sub debt" and increased its dividend.
  • Allowance for credit losses was "1.25%" at year-end.

Q&A

  • David Bishop, Hovde Group: Asked about payoff trends and loan demand. Broughton responded, "projected payoffs has dropped substantially quarter-over-quarter...at least it's trending in the right direction."
  • Bishop followed up on Texas team expectations. Broughton replied, "their budgeted growth for 2026 is higher than any other region...they're primarily C&I lenders."
  • Steve Moss, Raymond James: Inquired about margin sustainability and repricing opportunities. Sparacio answered, "using the December spot margin is a good starting point for 2026," and detailed "about $2 billion opportunity" for repricing loans in the next 12 months.
  • Moss also asked about the $5 million charge-off and multifamily nonperforming assets. Broughton confirmed, "the charge was related to the health care asset...largely has been put behind us now," while the multifamily issue is being worked through with the borrower.
  • Moss queried about expense growth, with Sparacio stating, "we're thinking high single digit" and emphasizing producer hires rather than back-office staff.
  • Moss asked about M&A disruptions and hiring. Broughton said, "we're going to hire as many good people as we can find."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts asked probing questions about payoff trends, loan demand, margin sustainability, credit quality, and Texas expansion, maintaining a neutral to slightly positive tone, seeking clarity but not expressing major concern.
  • Management's tone was confident during prepared remarks, particularly about margin expansion and Texas growth, and remained consistent during Q&A, providing detailed responses and reiterating optimism for 2026. Broughton stated, "we are pleased...with a good ending" to 2025 and expressed excitement for 2026.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone shifted from cautious—focused on loan paydowns and nonperformers—to more constructive, while management's confidence increased, especially regarding margin trajectory and strategic hires.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from cautious optimism on loan growth and margin in Q3 to more assertive targets for margin expansion and Texas-led growth in Q4.
  • Management focus moved from managing loan paydowns and addressing nonperformers in Q3 to executing on new market expansion and booking robust loan growth in Q4.
  • Analysts pivoted from concerns about credit quality and loan pipeline in Q3 to margin sustainability and the impact of new hires in Q4.
  • Key metrics improved: net interest margin expanded, efficiency ratio declined, and loan growth rebounded versus prior quarter.
  • Management expressed greater confidence in cost controls and organic growth, expecting new initiatives to drive performance in 2026.

Risks and Concerns

  • Credit quality risk was noted, with nonperforming assets rising to "97 basis points" of total assets, attributed to a single merchant developer exposure.
  • Management is actively managing problem loans, with Harper stating, "we continue to proactively manage our loan portfolio achieving a number of successful outcomes."
  • Expense growth risk from Texas expansion was acknowledged, with Sparacio noting the expectation for higher expenses but neutralizing impact with revenue growth.
  • Multifamily portfolio remains under close watch, as management works with borrowers for orderly resolution.

Final Takeaway

ServisFirst Bancshares concluded 2025 with strong momentum, highlighted by robust loan growth, expanding net interest margins, and disciplined cost control. With the addition of a new Texas team positioned for outsized growth in 2026 and a pipeline of repricing opportunities expected to support margin expansion, management expressed optimism for continued performance gains. Credit quality remains a focus, but proactive management and balanced portfolio growth strategies underpin a confident outlook as the company advances into the new year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: United Airlines rallies after saying it's on track for record profits
data.description:
United Airlines (UAL) rallied in postmarket trading on Tuesday after the airline company said it may churn up record earnings this year. The company's confident forecast was based on strong travel demand trends. Notably, United (UAL) said business bookings were up double digits over the last two weeks

For the full year, United (YUAL) sees EPS of $12 and $14 (midpoint $13.00) vs. the consensus estimate of $13.19. 

As part of its update, United (UAL) noted that it plans to enhance the customer experience as it plans to take delivery of over 100 narrowbody aircraft and approximately 20 Boeing 787 aircraft, which would be more widebody aircraft in a year than any U.S. passenger airline has had taken in since 1988. United (UAL) plans to use the new aircraft to profitably expand its network, including its industry-leading international routes and growing domestic network. In addition, the airline aims to make significant airport upgrades at its Washington Dulles and Houston hubs.

Shares of UAL rose 3.3% in postmarket trading.

data.title: Petrobras signs $521M in contracts for five gas carriers, other vessels
data.description:
Petrobras (PBR) signed contracts with shipyards for five gas carriers, 18 barges and 18 pushers for a combined 2.8B reais (~$521M), Reuters reported Tuesday.

A shipyard in Rio Grande do Sul will build the five tankers, representing 2.2B reais of the deal, to carry liquefied petroleum gas and derivatives, according to the report.

Two of the tankers will have a capacity of 14K cm, and the other three will be able to transport 7K cm each, with the first gas tanker scheduled for delivery within 33 months after construction begins and new deliveries every six months, the company said.

"With these contracts, we are preparing Petrobras for the growth of our production in the coming years and boosting the recovery of the national shipbuilding industry," Petrobras (PBR) CEO Magda Chambriard said.

Separately, Petrobras (PBR) said Friday it produced 2.4M bbl/day of oil and total oil and gas production reached 2.99M boe/day in FY 2025, both up 11% from 2024 and above the upper limit of the targets set by the company in its 2025-29 business plan.

data.title: Rio Tinto reports higher Q4 copper output as huge Mongolian mine ramps up
data.description:
Rio Tinto (RIO) exceeded expectations for quarterly iron ore and copper production, reporting Wednesday that iron ore production at its core Pilbara operations hit a record and copper output increased due to the continued expansion of its operation at Oyu Tolgoi in Mongolia.

Q4 shipments of iron ore, still Rio’s (RIO) biggest source of revenue by far, jumped 7% Y/Y to 91.3M metric tons, above the Visible Alpha consensus estimate of 88.2M tons, while output rose by 4% to 89.7M tons, but full-year exports of 326.2M tons fell 1% and came in near the lower end of its forecast range of 323M-338M tons, "with a strong recovery from the extreme weather interruptions earlier in the year."

The company said mined copper production on a consolidated basis rose 5% Y/Y to 240K metric tons, above the Visible Alpha consensus estimate of 214.4K tons, while annual output climbed 11% to 883K tons, topping its guidance of 860K-875K tons, driven by the continued underground ramp-up, higher grades and recovery rates at the Oyu Tolgoi mine.

Rio Tinto (RIO) disclosed earlier this month it is in talks with Glencore about a potential deal that could create the world's largest mining company, but the negotiations were not discussed in its quarterly update.

data.title: Energy Fuels to buy Australian Strategic Materials in $299M deal
data.description:
Energy Fuels (UUUU) +1.1% post-market Tuesday after saying it agreed to acquire rare earth metals producer Australian Strategic Materials (ASMMF) for US$299M, to be completed through a scheme of arrangement under Australian law.

Energy Fuels (UUUU) said it is creating what it believes will be the largest, fully integrated rare earth elements "mine-to-metal and alloy" producer outside of China to close a critical strategic gap in global supply chains for magnet applications.

The transaction will combine Australian Strategic Materials' operating Korean Metals Plant - one of the few facilities outside of China currently producing REE metals and alloys - and its planned American Metals Plant with Energy Fuels' (UUUU) existing REE oxide production at its White Mesa Mill in Utah, the only U.S. facility capable of separating monazite concentrates into both light and heavy REE oxides that are planned to be utilized in ASM's metallization and alloying facilities in South Korea and the U.S.

Energy Fuels (UUUU) rose more than 7% in regular trading as B. Riley raised its price target to $27 with a Buy rating on the shares.

data.title: Newegg Commerce down 13% after board chairman detention
data.description:
• Newegg Commerce (NEGG) said it has received a notice from the family of He Zhitao, the company’s controlling shareholder and chairman, that they received a “Notice of Detention” and a “Notice of Investigation” and that Zhitao is being placed under investigation and has been detained. 
  • Shares of the company were down 13% at $48.25 in extended trading on Tuesday.
  • Filing.

data.title: Gold seen climbing to $5,000, silver to sell off after hitting $100, BNP Paribas analyst says
data.description:
New geopolitical uncertainties including President Trump's new Greenland tariffs and concerns about the Federal Reserve's independence look primed to push gold prices to $5,000/oz sooner than expected, but the easing of physical dislocations should see silver sell off soon after reaching $100/oz, BNP Paribas Director of Commodities Strategy David Wilson said.

"Gold thrives on uncertainty," Wilson told Bloomberg on Monday. "We saw it through last year - we were obviously hitting highs all the way through last year - and continue to see that uncertainty at play now."

"Every type of support that you see for gold is at play at the moment, supporting the yellow metal," Wilson said. "We forecast back in November that we thought that gold was going to hit $5,000 sooner or later. It looked quite a big call back in November, [but] when you look at it now, we're at $4,700. $5,000 is not much of a stretch."

But silver is due for a correction, Wilson said, suggestiing that even as the metal continues to set new highs, the physical shortages that drove 2025's strong rally are resolving.

The physical market for silver is "definitely easing, lease rates are dropping back quite substantially, so those kinds of issues look like they're dissipating," and silver is a much thinner, less liquid market than gold, Wlson said. "As soon as some of the speculative flows start looking to take profits, then it's quite likely we could get quite a significant correction."

Gold and silver futures vaulted to new record highs Tuesday after President Trump ramped up his rhetoric about acquiring Greenland, with his threats of slapping tariffs on several European countries rekindling fears of a potential trade war.

Meanwhile, the U.S. dollar posted its largest daily decline in more than a month, making greenback-priced gold more affordable for overseas buyers.

President Trump's "latest escalation against NATO allies is likely to further shake market participants' confidence in the U.S. dollar as a safe haven and drive them to other safe havens," Commerzbank analyst Carsten Fritsch said in a note. "The ultimate safe haven is gold, which is largely immune to the influence of politicians and central banks."

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for January delivery closed +3.7% to $4,759.60/oz, the metal's largest one-day dollar gain on record and biggest one-day percentage increase since April 9, 2020, and front-month Comex January silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended +6.9% to $94.206/oz.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

  • Proceeds will fund working capital, RV resort developments, expansions, and acquisition op

data.title: Currenc Group receives Nasdaq notice over annual meeting compliance
data.description:
• Currenc Group (Nasdaq: CURR) on Tuesday said that it received a notice from Nasdaq that it is not in compliance with listing rules after failing to hold an annual shareholders’ meeting within 12 months of its fiscal year-end.
  • The Singapore-based fintech company said the notice, received on January 12, has no immediate effect on the listing or trading of its shares.
  • Currenc has 45 days to submit a plan to regain compliance.
  • The company said it plans to consult with Nasdaq on whether holding its annual meeting, scheduled for February 25, would restore compliance, and that Nasdaq could grant up to 180 days from the fiscal year-end to regain compliance.
  • CURR -2.66% after hours to $1.46.
  • Source: Press Release
